rationale: The citing paper uses Ghosh and John (2005) as a core prior study for its contingent alignment framing, explicitly discussing that paper's investment-governance alignment logic when motivating its own hypotheses. It repeatedly positions its contribution as advancing contingent alignment and governance value analysis in a new channel setting (PICs) with additional extradyadic factors. This is more than background listing because the cited work informs the conceptual setup and interpretation of the citing paper's contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Ghosh and John (2005) by transferring contingent alignment logic from industrial alliances to partially integrated channels and adding brand reputation and feedback-based contingencies for ongoing governance decisions. It builds on that prior alignment perspective rather than merely citing it as general TCA background.
